channel.title: Will L. Arnold III on Will Arnold
channel.description: Recent content in Will L. Arnold III on Will Arnold

item.title: Biodiversity in National Parks
item.description: Biodiversity Repository GitHub Intro Animal Data from the national parks was analyzed to find out more about what animals are endangered, where they are located and other variables that were correlative with the unfortunate status. Mammals and birds were most likely to need intervention. Birds and Myotis/Bats were not statistically significantly related together but they were also not statistically significantly related to other categories like reptiles and fish. Myotis and bats were overwhelmingly the most likely to need intervention of all of the other species.

item.title: Airline Analysis
item.description: Airline Repository GitHub 1. Intro This project is an analysis of flight data. There were 129,780 observations in this dataset. Variables in this data that were analyzed include coach price, length of flight, number of passengers, miles of flight, flight delay, as well as various different features of the flights and their relationship to price. It was desired to understand if $500 for a flight ticket would be unreasonable for an 8-hour flight.

item.title: Westinghouse
item.description: Description Conceptualized and Fabricated a Dashboard that streamlined collaboration between various departments. Inclusion of different statistical methods and visualizations that accelerated decision making and communication. In order to reduce machine down time in a manufacturing process. Adapted to independent work environment with minimal interaction with other software engineers during programming. SQL Querying data and imbedding logic to display active statistics that updated every 5 minutes from an oracle database. Python code that was responsible for managing the SQL query.

item.title: NASA Space Grant
item.description: Description Analyzed use of Epilog Lasers Zing 16 laser engraver and fabricated a tool that supported a more effective use of the machine. Assisting in improved quality of experience and decrease of test materials. Skills acquired and main takeaways Tool conceptualization Independent Project Orchestration Self Reliance

item.title: Sigmabrew
item.description: still tryna figure out how to display this one tbh

item.title: About me
item.description: What Led Me Here? College Coming out of high school, like many others, I lacked a sense of direction. I decided to major in Industrial Engineering due to the fact that it is a very broad subject and can lead to many different career paths. I started in the lowest math class the Engineering program allowed. I was projected to graduate is 6.5 years. X,Y, Input Output During my battles with the curriculum I received a strong base in statistics, simulation, calculus, hypothesis testing, operations research, and programming with python.

item.title: Interests
item.description: Mountain Biking Building Computers Dirt Biking College Football Go Mountaineers! Investing Movies/TV Music Lifting Weights Art Video Games Comic Books Regular Books lol Amateur Drone Photography Amateur Regular Photography

item.title: Skills
item.description: Current Skills Create Programs with Python 3 SQL oracle postgreSQL SQLite Summarize and analyze data sets Conduct Hypothesis Testing One Sample T-Test Binomial Linear Regression T-Test F-Test Data Manipulation w/ Pandas Statistics Web Scraping with Beautiful Soup Exploratory Data analysis Clean and Tidy Datasets w/ Pandas Visualization with seaborn, matplotlib Communicate Data Science Findings Future Skills Supervised ML Models Linear regression Logistic regression K nearest Neighbors Decision Trees Naive Bayes Classifiers Support Vector Machines Random Forests Unsupervised ML Models K-means Clustering Principle Compound analysis Deep Learning with Tensor Flow Regression Classification Feature engineering Data Transformation Feature Selection Evaluation Metrics Normalization Training, Validation, and Test Data sets Accuracy, Recall, Precision, F1 AUC, ROC Handling Big Data with PySpark
